title: calea scabra var. longifolia
intro: Calea scabra var. longifolia, also known as Mexican mint, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its distinctive long leaves and fragrant foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which can help to relieve anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. The health benefits of Mexican mint include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief and sleep disorders. Calea scabra var. longifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal combinations to enhance its therapeutic effects.
